nodes.context: Italian-American street crew from Boston's North End, active 1960s-mid 1980s, involved in racketeering, murder, extortion, gambling, loan sharking.
Italian-American Mafia family based in New England, of which the Angiulo brothers were made members.
Irish-American gang based in Somerville, Massachusetts, rivals of the Angiulo brothers.
Irish-American gang from Charlestown, Boston, mentioned as rival to Angiulo brothers.

edges.context: The Angiulo brothers were made men in the Patriarca crime family, placed in control of racketeering in Massachusetts.
The Winter Hill Gang decided to run rackets in their own neighborhoods, leading to conflict with the Angiulo brothers.
The Charlestown Mob, like the Winter Hill Gang, competed with the Angiulo brothers for control of rackets.
